sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Newcastle United come into this match with solid form, having won 3 of their last 5 matches, including a convincing 3-0 victory against Benfica. Athletic Club, on the other hand, have struggled recently, managing just 2 wins in their last 5 games, with a notable 3-1 win over Qarabağ FK but also a loss to Getafe.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In their last encounter in 2022, Newcastle United edged out Athletic Club with a 2-1 victory at St James' Park. This historical edge, combined with their home advantage, gives Newcastle a psychological boost for today's match.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Newcastle United have shown attacking prowess with 8 goals scored this season and rank highly in big chances created (14, 8th in league). Their defense is also strong, conceding just 2 goals (3rd best in league). Athletic Club, positioned 25th in the standings with only 3 points, have struggled defensively, conceding 7 goals in recent matches.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Newcastle's Aaron Ramsdale (9.4 avg. Sofascore rating) and Anthony Gordon (1 goal, 1 assist) are in top form, providing stability and flair. Athletic Club will rely on their attacking players to exploit any defensive lapses, but they lack standout performers in recent games.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Newcastle's high corner count (7.67 per game, 1st in league) and ability to score late (3 goals in 76-90 min) suggest they will dominate set-pieces and push for late goals. Athletic Club's tendency to concede first (8/10 matches) and struggle with clean sheets (3 games without one) could be exploited by Newcastle's early aggression.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Newcastle's superior form, home advantage, and Sofascore's 70% probability of a home win, they are clear favorites. Athletic Club's defensive vulnerabilities and lower league standing make an upset unlikely. Expect Newcastle to control possession and create more scoring opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Newcastle United hold a narrow 1-0 lead over Athletic Club at St James' Park. The home side took the advantage early through a headed goal by Dan Burn in the 11th minute. Newcastle have dominated possession and shown more intent in the final third, though they’ve missed a couple of big chances to extend their lead. Athletic Club, despite being behind, have had their moments and remain in the game with some promising entries into the penalty area.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Newcastle United largely dictating the attacking momentum with consistent pressure in the opening stages and a significant surge around the time of their goal. They maintained frequent attacking spells, particularly in the middle and latter parts of the half, showing intent to build on their lead. Athletic Club had notable periods of momentum, especially around the midpoint of the half, with sustained pressure that threatened to equalize, but they couldn’t convert their opportunities into goals. The momentum swung back to Newcastle towards the end of the half, suggesting they’ll look to start the second half on the front foot.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key team statistics
halftimeSections.text: Newcastle United have controlled the game with a significant advantage in ball possession and a high success rate in duels, particularly in the air. Their entries into the final third and penalty area reflect a more direct approach in attack, though they’ve been wasteful with big chances. Athletic Club, on the other hand, have struggled to win duels and aerial battles, which has limited their ability to build sustained pressure. Despite lower possession, they’ve managed a decent number of final third entries and penalty area penetrations, hinting at potential for a comeback if they can be more clinical.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Standout players
halftimeSections.text: For Newcastle, Dan Burn has been the standout performer, not only scoring the opening goal but also contributing defensively with key clearances and interceptions. Malick Thiaw has also been solid at the back with a high number of clearances, while Joelinton showed threat in attack despite missing a big chance. On Athletic Club’s side, Jesús Areso has been notable for his defensive work with multiple tackles won and interceptions, while Unai Gómez provided a spark in attack with a shot on target. However, the away side lacks a clear focal point in attack based on the first-half performance.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Newcastle United are likely to continue their high-possession approach, looking to exploit their dominance in duels to create more scoring opportunities. They may focus on converting their big chances, potentially bringing fresh legs in attack to maintain intensity. Athletic Club will need to improve their duel-winning percentage and capitalize on set-pieces or counter-attacks, given their higher number of corner kicks. They might push for more aggressive final third entries to test Newcastle’s defense, which has looked strong but not impenetrable.
